reasoning:
QCOM's baseline price on March 1, 2026 (based on the Feb 27, 2026 Friday close) is approximately $141.45. QCOM pays an $0.89 dividend with an ex-dividend date of March 5, 2026. This mechanical price drop of ~0.6% creates a clear headwind for the short-term March 8 resolution: the stock must organically climb more than 0.6% in just one week just to break even with the March 1 baseline. Given the recent negative price momentum calculated by the yfinance tool (downward slope of ~$0.51/day from December 2025 to late February 2026) and typical weekly volatility, the probability of overcoming this mechanical drop in 7 days is suppressed (estimated to be ~45%). 

For the March 31 resolution, the longer 30-day forecasting horizon increases uncertainty. As horizon increases, the random-walk nature of equities and the pervasive positive long-term market drift have more time to counter the initial ex-dividend drop. Therefore, the probability naturally reverts closer to 0.50. Given the lack of major upcoming targeted catalysts (the latest earnings call was Feb 4), I estimate the chance of resolving YES on March 31 at 49%.
